title: Lumen — a from-scratch path tracer
description: A physically based path tracer built from scratch in TypeScript. NEWEST (22.0) — THE CAMERA BECOMES PHYSICAL (A POST-CAPTURE IMAGE-FORMATION PIPELINE): for twenty-one versions Lumen got the SCENE-REFERRED RADIANCE exactly right — five integrators, spectral subsurface, a chromatic atmosphere, importance-sampled HDRIs — but a PHOTOGRAPH IS NOT THE RADIANCE FIELD; it is what a CAMERA AND A PIECE OF FILM did to it. 22.0 adds the rest of the imaging chain — POLYGONAL-APERTURE BOKEH, energy-conserving VEILING-GLARE BLOOM, natural cos⁴θ VIGNETTING, lateral CHROMATIC ABERRATION and photographic FILM GRAIN — and proves every part. The design splits by colour space the way a real chain does: glare and vignetting are RADIOMETRIC so they run in LINEAR HDR before tone mapping, while aberration and grain are artefacts of the RECORDING MEDIUM so they run on the TONE-MAPPED image after the curve; the four film effects live in a new pure postprocess.ts on the UI thread (never touching the integrator), and the one transport-adjacent change (aperture SHAPE) is gated so a circular iris is the historical concentric-disk sampler BIT-FOR-BIT. With every knob at zero the whole stage is a BIT-EXACT IDENTITY, so the default render and all 117 prior transport proofs are untouched. The bokeh: a real iris is a regular polygon of blades, so an out-of-focus highlight images as that polygon — the hexagonal/octagonal BOKEH BALL a perfect circle can never make — and sampleAperture draws a point AREA-UNIFORMLY over the n-gon (u₁·n splits into an equally-likely wedge index and a fractional part that, independent of the index for uniform u₁, becomes a barycentric coordinate via the (a,b)→(1−a,1−b) fold), so because the polygon is symmetric the MEAN LENS OFFSET IS ZERO and depth of field stays EXACTLY UNBIASED — only the SHAPE of the circle of confusion changes. The bloom: a passive optic neither creates nor destroys light, so glare is the convex blend (1−s)·image+s·glare(image) where glare is a NORMALISED multi-scale Gaussian PSF (a tight core + a broad veil, Spencer et al. 1995, each Gaussian three O(N) box passes) — the normalisation is what makes a centred highlight keep its TOTAL ENERGY. The vignette: the textbook cos⁴θ law 1/(1+r²)² tied to the lens field of view, the optical centre exactly unattenuated. The aberration: red and blue radially rescaled about the centre (green the reference channel), the centre a fixed point of every channel. The grain: a monochromatic ZERO-MEAN dither whose amplitude follows √(L(1−L)) so it peaks in the midtones and VANISHES at pure black and white, deterministic in the pixel coordinate so the thumbnail and verify suite are reproducible. Two showcase scenes — Neon Bokeh (a focused subject behind ~70 tiny neon motes at a wide depth spread + a 6-blade wide-open iris, every defocused mote a crisp hexagonal bokeh disc) and Lumière Hall (a dim corridor of fierce bulbs for energy-conserving glare and corner vignetting). Eight new proofs (118 total): the bokeh sampler is AREA-UNIFORM (the inscribed-circle fraction matches π·apothem²/area(n-gon)), inside the unit disk and ZERO-MEAN (unbiased DoF); it REDUCES to the disk sampler for blades<3 and FILLS the disk as blades→∞; glare is ENERGY-CONSERVING for a centred feature and an IDENTITY at strength 0; an impulse spreads into a MONOTONE-FALLOFF halo; the vignette is EXACTLY cos⁴θ, unit at the centre, monotone, identity off; chromatic aberration is an IDENTITY at the centre and at magnitude 0, leaves GREEN FIXED, and shifts red radially; film grain is ZERO-MEAN, fixes BLACK & WHITE, identity off, variance rising with strength; and the WHOLE PIPELINE AT ZERO IS A BIT-EXACT IDENTITY. Also (21.0) — IMAGE-BASED LIGHTING (HDRI ENVIRONMENT IMPORTANCE SAMPLING): for twenty versions Lumen's environment was a constant colour, a vertical gradient, or the analytic Preetham sky, and the ONLY part of it next-event estimation could sample was the SUN — a single small cone; everything else (a softbox, a sunset horizon, a band of city lights) was found only by a BSDF ray that happened to point at it, so a glossy surface under a vivid structured environment rendered as a storm of colour noise. 21.0 brings the production-standard answer — an EQUIRECTANGULAR HDRI environment, IMPORTANCE SAMPLED in full: a from-scratch implementation of PBRT's InfiniteAreaLight (envmap.ts) builds a PIECEWISE-CONSTANT 2D DISTRIBUTION from every texel's LUMINANCE × sinθ (the lat-long map's solid-angle Jacobian) and samples it by a MARGINAL-then-CONDITIONAL inverse CDF (Distribution1D/Distribution2D), returning a direction toward the BRIGHT parts of the panorama with an EXACT SOLID-ANGLE pdf p(ω)=p(u,v)/(2π²·sinθ) that MIS-pairs with BSDF sampling byte-for-byte the way every other Lumen light does — so the estimator stays PROVABLY UNBIASED and only the variance collapses (a ~22× per-sample variance drop on a sunset environment at an identical mean, and effectively unbounded on the features BSDF sampling can't find). It is a drop-in for the ENVIRONMENT SLOT ONLY: a new EnvDef 'hdri' variant builds an EnvMap in the Scene constructor, envRadiance reads it, the env slot of the NEE pool samples it by importance instead of as a sun cone, and envSunPdf returns its density for the MIS weight; the env-slot count is generalised from envSun to hasEnvLight (IDENTICAL for the old env kinds), so every non-HDRI scene is reproduced BIT-FOR-BIT and all 104 prior proofs stay green — the path tracer, the path-guided tracer and Metropolis all inherit the win. The panoramas are GENERATED PROCEDURALLY (no image assets, deterministic across the worker pool and the verify suite): Studio (a dark stage lit by three soft rectangular sources — the product-shot setup where most of the dome is black), Sunset (a graded sky with a blinding low sun carrying most of the energy) and Twilight (a deep dome over a horizon strewn with ~520 warm city lights and a moon — the many-tiny-emitters regime, breathtaking in chrome), with a live ROTATION knob that spins the panorama (the importance distribution rides with it, proven a measure-preserving symmetry) and an INTENSITY knob that scales radiance without touching the pdf. Three showcase scenes lit by the ENVIRONMENT ALONE — Studio HDRI, Sunset HDRI, Twilight HDRI. Six new proofs (110 total): the 2D distribution is a genuine density (∫∫p du dv=1 exactly AND the directional ∫_S² p dω=1 by Monte-Carlo); the importance sampler MATCHES pdf(wi) to MACHINE ε with every direction unit and positive; a CONSTANT environment REDUCES TO UNIFORM 1/(4π) over the sphere (the equatorial-Jacobian oracle, the IBL analogue of the light tree's coincident-lights ⇒ 1/N); MIS CONSISTENCY through the Scene (envSunPdf ≡ EnvMap.pdf/numLights ≡ the NEE sampler's returned pdf — the no-double-count guarantee); the importance estimator is UNBIASED (means agree to <1%) with a ~22× lower variance than uniform on a peaked environment; and env ROTATION is a measure-preserving symmetry (the same deviates give identical radiance and pdf, the azimuth advanced by exactly φ). Also (20.0) — SPHERE-LIGHT NEXT-EVENT ESTIMATION (subtended-cone sampling): for nineteen versions Lumen's NEE could connect a shadow ray only to TRIANGLE emitters — a flat triangle has an exact solid-angle pdf d²/(cosθ·A), so its MIS weights are provably consistent, but an emissive SPHERE has no such pdf and was left to BSDF sampling alone (a scattered ray that happens to strike it), which finds a small bright orb a fraction of a percent of the time, so a sphere-lit room rendered as a STORM OF FIREFLIES — the very premise of the Glowing Orb scene. 20.0 closes that gap with the textbook estimator (PBRT 'Sampling Spheres'): the directions from a shade point that hit a sphere of radius R a distance d away form a CONE of half-angle θmax with cosθmax=√(1−R²/d²), and sampling UNIFORMLY inside that cone gives a constant solid-angle density p(ω)=1/Ω with Ω=2π(1−cosθmax) — so every shadow ray lands ON the orb and none is wasted on the dark around it, with the near-hit distance computed in CLOSED FORM (d·cosθ−√(R²−d²sin²θ), robust at the grazing cone edge). The SAME Ω drives the MIS weight when a BSDF ray instead lands on the sphere (sphereDirPdf), so the two estimators stay consistent and the result is PROVABLY UNBIASED — only the variance collapses. It is a drop-in for the SELECTION STEP ONLY (mirroring the 14.0 light tree): sampleLight/lightPdf/envSunPdf learn a useSphere flag, the emissive spheres join the uniform 1/N selection pool as a residual slot beside the light tree's triangle mass and the env sun, and the integrator is otherwise untouched — so the surface NEE, the VOLUMETRIC in-scattering NEE (a medium can now connect to a sphere), and the path tracer / path-guided / Metropolis estimators all inherit the win. It is gated behind a 'Sphere lights (cone NEE)' toggle (default off, on for two new scenes), and with it off every code path is the historical one BIT-FOR-BIT, so all 98 prior proofs stay green; a shade point INSIDE a sphere has no subtending cone, so the sampler declines there and BSDF sampling carries it unbiasedly. Two showcase scenes — Plasma Lamps (a still life lit only by three vivid emissive bulbs of varied size/distance, with sharp coloured reflections in a polished steel sphere) and Firefly Swarm (~50 tiny bright motes over a meadow, the sphere-light analogue of Star Field). Six new proofs (104 total): the subtended-cone solid angle equals 2π(1−cosθmax) and REDUCES to the inverse-square πR²/d² as R/d→0 (the point-light limit); the cone sampler MATCHES its pdf (∫_cone dω=Ω by Monte-Carlo, every sampled ray actually intersects the sphere, pdf≡1/Ω); the directional pdf INTEGRATES TO 1 over the sphere of directions (a genuine probability distribution ⇒ unbiased); the headline — the cone-NEE estimator matches the ANALYTIC SPHERE FORM FACTOR ρ·L·sin²θmax·cosθc to under a standard error while its per-sample VARIANCE is over 100,000× below the naive hemisphere sampler (the firefly-storm regime removed); MIS CONSISTENCY (the sampler's pdf equals scene.lightPdf for the same direction to machine ε, the no-double-count guarantee that keeps the BSDF-hit MIS weight exact); and the estimator respects the HORIZON (a sub-floor sphere leaks nothing) and DECLINES INSIDE a sphere. Also (19.0) — AgX TONE MAPPING: the final step — squeezing the unbounded HDR buffer into a monitor's 0–1 — is a real image-formation choice, and Lumen's old operators (ACES, Reinhard, Hejl filmic) were all PER-CHANNEL, which has a notorious failure: a bright saturated colour drives one channel to clip while the others lag, so the hue SKEWS (an intense blue light turns magenta, a fire's core goes pure red). 19.0 adds AgX (Troy Sobotka's transform, now the Blender default), which couples the channels so highlights DESATURATE TOWARD WHITE as they brighten, the way film and the eye do: it rotates linear colour through a desaturated inset matrix, compresses luminance over a fixed log₂ window, applies a sigmoidal contrast curve (Wrensch's 6th-order fit), then rotates back through the outset matrix. It is purely a DISPLAY transform — it runs over the averaged buffer and never touches the unbiased light transport, so it cannot perturb any of the 94 existing proofs — selectable beside ACES/Filmic/Reinhard/Linear. Four new proofs (98 total): the contrast curve is monotone and bounded; a NEUTRAL GREY STAYS NEUTRAL (the row-stochastic inset/outset preserve the white point — the guard that caught the column-major matrix transpose, with 18% grey landing on display byte 128); black→black with luminance monotone in exposure; and a blinding saturated colour provably desaturates toward white. Also (18.0) — PHYSICALLY BASED LIGHT COLOUR (BLACKBODY EMITTERS): a real light source has a TEMPERATURE, not an RGB colour — a tungsten lamp is ~2700 K and warm, daylight ~6500 K and neutral, a clear north sky ~10000 K and blue, the warm→cool sweep every photographer knows as colour temperature. That progression is the PLANCKIAN LOCUS, fixed by physics, and Lumen now computes it from scratch: planck(λ,T) is Planck's law for spectral radiance, integrated against the CIE 1931 COLOUR-MATCHING FUNCTIONS (the analytic Wyman–Sloan–Shirley fit, no 1 nm table) to get the tristimulus the eye sees, then converted XYZ→linear-sRGB — so a scene specifies a light the way a real one is rated, blackbody(3200), and gets the physically correct hue. It needs no integrator or material change (a colour helper computed at scene-build time) yet is as grounded as the transport it feeds. New Colour Temperature scene — a row of panels from 2000 K to 12000 K washing neutral spheres, the locus read straight off physically-coloured light. Four new proofs (94 total): Planck positivity + WIEN'S DISPLACEMENT (numerically located peak λ_max·T ≈ 2.898×10⁶ nm·K to <1%); STEFAN–BOLTZMANN (band integral ∝ T⁴ ⇒ ∫B(2T)/∫B(T) ≈ 16); the locus runs warm→neutral→cool with a strictly monotone red/blue ratio, every hue bounded in [0,1]; and 6500 K lands on a near-neutral white point (the calibration anchor of the whole Planck→CMF→XYZ→sRGB pipeline). Also (17.0) — A SHARPER LIGHT TREE (SAH SPLITTING + RECEIVER-AWARE IMPORTANCE): two refinements to the 14.0 light BVH that the many-lights paper (Conty Estevez & Kulla 2018) builds in. First, the tree is now built with a SURFACE-AREA HEURISTIC — instead of splitting each cluster of emitters at the median, it minimises Σ power·surfaceArea over every candidate split (a prefix/suffix sweep in O(count)), the light-transport analogue of the geometry BVH's SAH, so a lone bright lamp is isolated from a halo of dim ones and each descent's importance estimate is sharper. Second, the cluster importance is now RECEIVER-AWARE: it folds the shade point's SURFACE NORMAL into the weight (a floored cone bound on how much of the cluster's box lies in the lit hemisphere), so a cluster tucked BEHIND the surface — which the cosine term will zero out anyway — is heavily down-weighted instead of drawing half the shadow rays. Both stay UNBIASED (the selection pdf is still renormalised at every split, and a floor keeps every light strictly positive), threaded through sampleLight/lightPdf and kept MIS-consistent via the stored vertex normal, so the image is unchanged and only the noise drops; a receiver normal is optional everywhere, so volume/subsurface scatter vertices and every prior proof use the receiver-agnostic path verbatim. New Light Cage scene — a faceted object inside a full sphere of ~220 inward-facing lights, half of them behind any facet's normal, the regime where the receiver term wins most. Four new proofs (90 total): the receiver-aware pdf still sums to 1 for any point AND normal; its sampler matches its pdf; the receiver term provably steers selection mass to the lit hemisphere (a front cluster beats a coincident, equally-oriented back one) while staying normalised and positive; and on a sphere of lights half behind the receiver the receiver-aware and agnostic trees agree in the MEAN (unbiased) while the receiver-aware VARIANCE is clearly lower. Also (16.0) — CHROMATIC PARTICIPATING MEDIA (WHY THE SKY IS BLUE): a real atmosphere does not extinguish every colour equally — Rayleigh scattering strips BLUE from a beam far faster than red (σ_t ∝ ~1/λ⁴), which is exactly why the sky is blue and the setting sun is red. Lumen's media carried a SCALAR extinction (one σ_t for every colour), so a haze or cloud could only be tinted by what it SCATTERED, never by HOW FAR EACH COLOUR REACHED. 16.0 adds a CHROMATIC EXTINCTION (a per-wavelength σ_t on the medium) traced with the same hero-wavelength trick the rest of the renderer uses: a path entering the medium commits to one wavelength λ, takes its RGB weight ONCE (E_λ[w]=(1,1,1), so the estimator stays UNBIASED), then DELTA-TRACKS the free-flight and RATIO-TRACKS the shadow transmittance against THAT wavelength's σ_t(λ) — the colour reconstructing over many paths. Because both null-collision estimators only ever needed a scalar σ_t and a constant majorant, it is a one-line σ_t→σ_t(λ) substitution that works for BOTH homogeneous (analytic) and heterogeneous (fBm cloud/smoke) media; the heterogeneous density shape is untouched, only the extinction SCALE is chromatic. The scalar path is preserved bit-for-bit so all 81 prior proofs stay green. Two new scenes — Rayleigh Haze (a warm sun reddening through a scattering atmosphere whose blue σ_t is ~6× its red, ringed by a blue single-scatter halo) and Amber Smoke (a plume coloured PURELY by its chromatic extinction over a neutral-grey albedo). Five new proofs (86 total): the per-wavelength homogeneous transmittance is EXACTLY e^(−σ_t(λ)L) (red transmits more than blue); ratio tracking through a constant field is unbiased at every λ; a chromatic pure-scattering furnace still returns EXACTLY 1 for ANY spectral σ_t (energy conservation, the unbiasedness oracle for the wavelength-resolved delta-tracking walk); a pure-absorbing haze reconstructs the SPECTRAL TRANSMITTANCE INTEGRAL (1/Δλ)∫w(λ)·e^(−σ_t(λ)·2r)dλ matched to a fine quadrature and exits R>G>B; and an achromatic chromatic-medium collapses onto the scalar medium. Also (15.0) — SPECTRAL SUBSURFACE SCATTERING (A CHROMATIC MEAN FREE PATH): the reason a hand held over a torch glows deep red at the edges is NOT that red is absorbed less per bounce — it is that red light TRAVELS FURTHER inside the flesh before it is absorbed at all. In skin, marble and milk the EXTINCTION ITSELF is chromatic (red low, blue high), so red reaches the thin edges while blue scatters back out near the surface. Lumen's 12.0 subsurface walk had a SCALAR mean free path — one σ_t for every colour — so all the chromatic character had to come from the per-collision albedo; it could not express this. 15.0 adds it by reusing the HERO-WAVELENGTH machinery that already disperses glass and colours real metals: a path that refracts into a SPECTRAL interior commits to one wavelength λ, takes its RGB weight ONCE (E_λ[w]=(1,1,1), so the estimator stays UNBIASED — colour is only a spread across λ, never a change of mean), then random-walks MONOCHROMATICALLY with the extinction σ_t(λ) and single-scattering albedo ϖ(λ) resolved at that wavelength, the chromatic translucency reconstructing over many paths' wavelengths. Nothing in the transport loop changes except WHICH scalar σ_t/ϖ the interior free-flight uses, so the scalar 12.0 walk is kept bit-for-bit for any non-spectral interior and all 75 prior proofs stay green. The media are not hand-tuned: a new MEASURED BSSRDF LIBRARY carries the canonical coefficients of Jensen, Marschner, Levoy & Hanrahan (SIGGRAPH 2001, Table 2) — marble, skin, whole & skim milk, cream, ketchup, chicken, potato, apple — converting their reduced σ_s′ and absorption σ_a into the per-wavelength extinction the walk consumes, so the new Living Skin (a measured-skin figurine raked from behind so its thin edges bleed red) and Apothecary (a shelf of spheres of real milk/marble/ketchup/cream, no two alike) scenes render REAL measured data. Six new proofs (81 total): the RGB→wavelength upsampling reproduces its control points and stays in envelope; every measured medium decodes to σ_t>0, ϖ∈[0,1] with red the deepest-penetrating for skin/marble; a chromatic pure-scatter furnace still returns EXACTLY 1 for ANY spectral σ_t (energy conservation, the unbiasedness oracle for the wavelength-resolved walk); a pure-absorbing slab reconstructs the SPECTRAL BEER INTEGRAL (1/Δλ)∫w(λ)·e^(−σ_t(λ)·2r)dλ matched to a fine deterministic quadrature and exits R>G>B (chromatic penetration, rendered); a spectral interior with achromatic parameters collapses exactly onto the scalar 12.0 walk (the reduction oracle); and a measured skin slab glows red end-to-end. Also (14.0) — IMPORTANCE SAMPLING OF MANY LIGHTS (THE LIGHT BVH): next-event estimation has to choose WHICH emitter to connect a shadow ray to, and Lumen used to pick one UNIFORMLY — perfect for one ceiling light, catastrophic for hundreds, where almost every shadow ray is spent on a far, occluded or back-facing emitter and the floor renders as a storm of colour noise. 14.0 fixes the distribution (not the sample count) with the production-standard answer — a LIGHT BVH (Conty Estevez & Kulla 2018, the technique now in Arnold and PBRT-v4): a binary tree over the emissive triangles whose every node caches its TOTAL POWER, its BOUNDING BOX, and a BOUNDING CONE OF EMITTER NORMALS. To pick a light for a shade point p, the sampler walks the tree from the root, at each step choosing the child in proportion to a cheap, conservative estimate of how much that CLUSTER could light p — importance = power·orientation(p)/distance²(p,box) — accumulating the product of branch probabilities as the EXACT selection pdf, which the MIS weight reuses; so near, bright, well-oriented lights win the lottery and the shadow rays land where the light actually is. It is a drop-in for the SELECTION STEP ONLY: sampleLight/lightPdf keep their contract, so the NEE↔BSDF MIS is untouched and the estimator stays UNBIASED — the tree only reshapes the variance of next-event estimation, never its mean, so a tree-sampled render converges to the SAME image a uniform one does, it just gets there far faster. Three guarantees make that airtight and PROVEN in the verify suite: the per-light selection probabilities SUM TO 1 at every p (a probability tree, normalised at each split); EVERY light keeps a strictly positive probability (a floored orientation term + clamped distance ⇒ no contributing light is ever excluded, the precondition for unbiasedness); and the SAMPLER MATCHES THE PDF to Monte-Carlo precision (the same branch probabilities drive sample and prob). It reduces EXACTLY to the uniform sampler when the clusters carry equal importance (a coincident-lights oracle nails 1/N to machine ε), and on a many-lights scene it cuts the NEE variance by HUNDREDS OF TIMES at equal mean. The uniform path is kept byte-for-byte as the default so every prior proof stays green; the tree is an opt-in toggle, on by default for two new showcase scenes — Star Field (a canopy of ~240 jewel-coloured emitters over a plaza) and Lantern Hall (a colonnade of warm lanterns where only the nearest few matter at any wall point). BDPT and the photon mapper sample lights their own way and are unaffected; the path tracer, the path-guided tracer and Metropolis all inherit the win. Five new proofs (75 total). Also (13.0) — PRACTICAL PATH GUIDING (THE SD-TREE): a FIFTH light-transport integrator that LEARNS its sampling distribution online (Müller, Gross & Novák 2017). As paths are traced, the radiance each carries is recorded into an SD-tree — a binary SPATIAL k-d tree over the scene whose every leaf owns a DIRECTIONAL quadtree over the sphere of directions, built on an EQUAL-AREA sphere↔square map so its area measure is proportional to solid angle with a constant Jacobian (a flux-proportional quadtree is therefore a radiance-proportional sampler with no cosine warp to undo). Both trees refine between iterations (each renders twice the samples of the last): the spatial tree subdivides where many paths PASS (a visit counter, so it grows even before any light is found — from 2 leaves to hundreds) and the directional tree sharpens where a bright direction APPEARS. At each surface the next direction is drawn from the MIXTURE α·p_bsdf+(1−α)·p_guide and weighted by that exact density, with the next-event-estimation and emission MIS weights using the same mixture pdf so every estimator stays consistent. Because the guide is a genuine probability distribution over the sphere (its density INTEGRATES TO 1, proven in the verify suite) the estimator is UNBIASED for any learned distribution — guiding only reshapes the variance, never the mean, so Guided converges to the SAME image as the other four (proven by a Guided≡path-tracer oracle on the diffuse box). Its home turf is light next-event estimation cannot sample: the new Glowing Orb scene is lit only by an emissive SPHERE (Lumen's NEE samples only triangle lights, so it is invisible to it) — blind BSDF sampling finds it ~0.5% of the time and the plain path tracer is all fireflies, while the guide learns the orb's direction per region (~9% hit rate) and renders it with measurably lower error at equal samples. Three engineering choices make it a real win rather than a naive net-negative: per-leaf MATURITY GATING (an under-trained quadtree samples nearly uniformly, which is worse than cosine BSDF sampling, so a region only guides once mature, else falls back to BSDF while still recording), VISIT-DRIVEN spatial subdivision, and a gentle α=0.7 (the piecewise-constant guide is a noisy sampler, so a 30% nudge atop 70% BSDF captures the win while bounding the variance, so guiding is never meaningfully worse than plain PT). Four new correctness proofs (DTree density ∫=1 over the sphere, sampler↔pdf to machine ε, ~100× variance cut on a peaked directional integral, and Guided≡PT on the box). Also (12.0) — SUBSURFACE SCATTERING: a dielectric can now carry an interior SCATTERING medium (scalar extinction σ_t, per-channel single-scattering albedo, Henyey–Greenstein g), turning glass into a TRANSLUCENT SOLID — marble, jade, wax, skin — that light refracts INTO, RANDOM-WALKS through and glows back OUT of. While the path is inside such a solid the integrator runs a homogeneous interior free-flight (distance ∼ e^(−σ_t·t)) bounded by the REAL surface geometry: a collision scatters off the phase function and multiplies throughput by the single-scattering albedo (so 1−albedo is absorbed at each step and the per-channel albedo IS the translucency's hue — why marble is warm-white and jade is green); reaching the boundary refracts the path out through the dielectric's exact Fresnel interface or TOTAL-INTERNALLY-REFLECTS it back in, trapping light to bounce again. It reuses the existing volume distance sampler, HG phase function and dielectric BSDF verbatim, so the transport loop is unchanged and all 61 prior proofs stay bit-for-bit green. The unmistakable subsurface glow a surface BRDF can never fake — the rim stays lit, the shadow side warms from within. Two showcase scenes (Subsurface Studio — a back-lit row of marble/jade/honey-wax/rose-quartz spheres; Jade Idol — a watertight hand-turned translucent lathe figurine) and FIVE correctness proofs: a pure-scattering index-matched furnace returns EXACTLY 1 for any phase g (zero variance — every path yields 1, proving the interior walk is unbiased and lossless), a pure-absorbing interior reproduces Beer's law e^(−σ·2r) straight from the free-flight sampler, the WHOLE translucent object (Fresnel boundary + TIR + multiple internal scattering) conserves energy to ≈1, a per-channel albedo tints the exitance R>G>B (the marble/jade/skin mechanism), and the reflectance rises strictly monotonically with the interior albedo — all bounded ≤1. Also (11.0) — REAL METALS FROM MEASURED COMPLEX IOR: a conductor's colour is not an RGB tint but a spectral reflectance set by its complex refractive index n̄(λ)=η(λ)−ik(λ), so Lumen now carries measured η/k spectra (gold/silver/copper from Johnson & Christy, aluminium from Rakić, iron & chromium) and evaluates the EXACT unpolarised conductor Fresnel at the path's committed hero wavelength — the same machinery that disperses glass — so a metal's hue emerges spectrally: gold rims warm, copper reads red, silver stays bright-neutral and aluminium leans faintly blue, with the correct grazing desaturation that Schlick-from-RGB can never capture. The complex-IOR Fresnel rides on every existing lobe (single-scatter GGX, anisotropic brushed metal, AND the Kulla–Conty multiscatter compensation) through one shared FresnelSpec, gated behind an optional spectrum field so the prior 55 proofs stay bit-for-bit green. New Metals of the World scene + six proofs — R∈[0,1] & grazing→1, the k→0 limit reducing EXACTLY to the dielectric Fresnel, the textbook metal colours, a furnace render reconstructing the measured RGB head-on, sampler↔pdf↔weight MIS consistency, and spectral multiscatter energy bounded by the hemispherical-average reflectance F̄. Also (10.0) — a physically based MATERIAL SYSTEM that makes the surfaces as rigorous as the light transport: ENERGY-CONSERVING ROUGH METAL via a Kulla–Conty multiple-scattering compensation lobe (driven by the GGX directional albedo E(μ,α) Monte-Carlo–integrated into a table at start-up) so rough gold stops darkening to grey and stays bright and saturated — proven in-app to reflect ≈1 in a white furnace with compensation and measurably less without; ANISOTROPIC (brushed/milled) METAL with two GGX roughness axes in a rotatable tangent frame so the highlight stretches into a streak, reciprocity and energy preserved exactly; OREN–NAYAR rough-diffuse for chalk/clay/plaster/the-moon flat-matte look; and CLEAR-COAT layered materials (glazed ceramic, lacquer, car paint) — a GGX dielectric coat over a Lambert/Oren–Nayar base, the two lobes sampled by Fresnel weight with a combined pdf so the stack conserves energy AND stays MIS-consistent (the new BSDFs match BDPT pixel-for-pixel in the verify suite). Three new showcase scenes — Brushed Metal, Rough Conductors (a single-scatter vs multiscatter split), and Ceramics & Clay — plus six new correctness proofs. It carries FIVE selectable light-transport integrators: a unidirectional Monte-Carlo path tracer, the path-guided path tracer above (the learned SD-tree), a full bidirectional path tracer (BDPT) — Veach–Guibas camera×light vertex connections weighted by balance-heuristic multiple importance sampling, which renders indirect-lit scenes (an emitter hidden in a cove) at a fraction of the noise — primary-sample-space Metropolis light transport (PSSMLT), a Markov chain run over the path tracer's own random-number stream (Kelemen mutation + Metropolis-Hastings acceptance, bootstrap normalisation, expected-value splatting, parallel chains across the worker pool) that locks onto the hardest-to-find light and refines the whole frame at once — the path tracer is reused verbatim as the contribution function because the Metropolis sampler simply IS the RNG — AND stochastic progressive photon mapping (SPPM), which runs the transport BACKWARDS to resolve the CAUSTICS the other three sample terribly (light focused through glass onto a floor — a light→specular→diffuse path next-event estimation can't connect through the lens): photons are emitted from the area lights (power-sampled to equal flux), refracted through the specular geometry and deposited at per-pixel measurement points via an exact CSR spatial hash, while a jittered camera pass each iteration places those points and the per-point gather radius is shrunk on the Hachisuka schedule R²←R²·(N+αM)/(N+M) so the density-estimation bias provably vanishes and the estimate converges. Because all four estimators solve the same rendering equation, the in-app verification suite proves they converge to the SAME image (checking PSSMLT and SPPM against the path tracer on both global brightness and spatial distribution), that SPPM resolves a finite focused caustic the others miss, that the photon-gather grid returns exactly the in-radius points vs brute force, and that the MIS weights form an exact partition of unity. The photon mapper is also SPECTRAL — a photon commits a random hero wavelength on its first dispersive hit so the caustic it focuses through glass fans into a RAINBOW (proven to conserve total energy while spreading into colour) — and DAYLIGHT-complete: the sun is itself a photon emitter (photons leave a disc sized to the scene's bounding sphere as a parallel beam), so daylight scenes get photon-mapped SUN CAUSTICS, with the distant-light flux normalisation proven against the path tracer. Showcase scenes include a Caustic Pool — a sine-ripple water surface (analytic smooth normals) throwing the shifting bright filaments of a swimming pool. Plus participating media (fog/smoke/cloud) with Henyey-Greenstein scattering and volumetric god rays, thin-film interference for soap-bubble/oil-slick iridescence, smooth triangle meshes (icosphere, lathe & OBJ import) with barycentric-interpolated normals, a SAH bounding volume hierarchy, GGX microfacet BSDFs with VNDF sampling, smooth and frosted dielectrics, spectral dispersion (prism rainbows), Beer-Lambert volumetric absorption, procedural textures, an analytic Preetham daylight sky whose sun is a sampled light, next-event estimation with multiple importance sampling, scrambled-Halton low-discrepancy sampling, adaptive variance-guided sampling with a live noise heatmap, a multi-threaded Web Worker pool, ACES tone mapping, and an edge-avoiding À-Trous denoiser — global illumination on the CPU, in your browser. The participating media are now HETEROGENEOUS too: procedural 3D fBm density fields (cumulus clouds, rising smoke plumes, exponential ground-fog layers) traced unbiasedly with the null-collision estimators — DELTA TRACKING (Woodcock free-flight sampling against a constant majorant, accepting a real scatter with probability σ(x)/σ̄) for in-volume collisions and RATIO TRACKING for shadow-ray transmittance — so a cloud self-shadows into soft greys, flares a forward-scattering silver lining toward the sun, and breaks light into real volumetric beams, all from one continuously varying field, with five new proofs (delta tracking reproduces e^(−σL), ratio tracking matches a fine quadrature of a varying optical depth, and a heterogeneous pure-scattering volume conserves energy exactly). Media can also EMIT light volumetrically — at a real collision the path collects (1−albedo)·Lₑ of self-radiance, so a heterogeneous field glows brightest in its dense core (a soft, physically integrated fireball, not a billboard), proven to obey the emission–absorption law (1−e^(−σ_t·chord))·Lₑ; see the glowing Ember scene.
